title: plagiobothrys procumbens
intro: Plagiobothrys procumbens, also known as creeping kidney-vetch or procumbent prickle-poppy, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the borage family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits associated with Plagiobothrys procumbens include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, teas, or tinctures, and its flowers and leaves can be dried and used in various forms to harness its medicinal properties.
